questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the power of judicial review?
questions.explanation: It gives the power to the Supreme Court to declare laws as unconstitutional.

questions.question: The concept of 'patriot game' is associated with the movement of?
questions.explanation: The movement of the tea workers in Assam and Bengal in 1946.

questions.question: Who is the first Chief Minister of West Bengal after independence?
questions.explanation: Bidhan Chandra Roy, a renowned physician, served as the first Chief Minister of West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following is a fundamental duty as per the Indian Constitution?
options: To promote harmony and the spirit of common brotherhood amongst all people residing in the territory of India
To preserve and improve the natural environment including forests, lakes, rivers and wild life, and to have compassion for living creatures
To strive towards excellence and self-improvement in all spheres of individual and collective life
To defend the country and render national service when called upon to do so
questions.explanation: It emphasizes the importance of promoting harmony and brotherhood among all people in India.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment to the Indian Constitution added which article?
questions.explanation: It deals with the powers, authority, and responsibilities of the Panchayats.

questions.question: Which river originates from the Himalayas and flows through the Darjeeling district in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It originates from the Chola Range of the Himalayas.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest mangrove forest in the world, located in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Sundarbans is the largest mangrove forest in the world.

questions.question: Which of the following mountains is the highest peak in the Chota Nagpur Plateau in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It is located in Giridih district of Jharkhand.

questions.question: Which river forms the border between West Bengal and Bangladesh?
questions.explanation: Padma River is a major distributary of the Ganges River.

questions.question: Which of the following cities is the administrative headquarters of the Cooch Behar district in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Cooch Behar is a city in the Cooch Behar district.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is launched by the Government of West Bengal to provide financial assistance to the women of the state?
questions.explanation: It aims to provide financial assistance to women for their self-employment.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Unnayan scheme of the Government of West Bengal?
options: To provide financial assistance to the economically backward classes
To promote entrepreneurship among the youth
To provide skill development training to the unemployed
To provide free education to the children of the state
questions.explanation: It aims to provide skill development training to the unemployed.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is a flagship initiative of the Government of West Bengal to provide health insurance coverage to the citizens of the state?
questions.explanation: It aims to provide health insurance coverage to the citizens of the state.

questions.question: What is the main focus area of the Sakshar Bangla Mission of the Government of West Bengal?
options: To provide financial assistance to the women of the state
To promote entrepreneurship among the youth
To provide skill development training to the unemployed
To improve literacy rate in the state
questions.explanation: It aims to improve literacy rate in the state.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is a initiative of the Government of West Bengal to provide housing assistance to the economically weaker sections of the society?
questions.explanation: It aims to provide housing assistance to the economically weaker sections of the society.

questions.question: A merchant from Kolkata buys a saree for ₹ 800. He sells it at a 20% profit. What is the selling price of the saree?
questions.explanation: The selling price is the cost price plus the profit.

questions.question: A local grocery store in Siliguri has a sale where items are offered at 15% discount. If an item originally costs ₹ 500, how much will you save?
questions.explanation: The discount amount is calculated as a percentage of the original price.

questions.question: A person buys 120 quintals of potatoes at ₹ 1200 per quintal. If he sells 100 quintals at a 12.5% loss, what is the selling price per quintal?
questions.explanation: The selling price is the cost price minus the loss amount.

questions.question: A shopkeeper from Asansol buys a cycle for ₹ 8000 and sells it for ₹ 9600. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: The profit percentage is calculated as the profit divided by the cost price.

questions.question: A person buys a bag for ₹ 1000 and sells it for ₹ 900. What is the percentage loss?
questions.explanation: The loss percentage is calculated as the loss divided by the cost price.

questions.question: A pipe fills a tank in 6 hours. How long will it take for 3 such pipes to fill the same tank?
questions.explanation: Work done by 3 pipes in 1 hour is 3 times the work done by 1 pipe in 1 hour.

questions.question: In a school, 8 boys can make 1400 copies in 4 hours. How many boys are needed to make 2100 copies in 3 hours?
questions.explanation: The number of copies made per hour by a boy is 8/4 * 1400 = 3500.

questions.question: A group of 6 men can finish a work in 10 days. If 3 more men join them, then how many days will they take to finish the same work?
questions.explanation: The total work decreases while the number of workers increases.

questions.question: A man can complete a job in 10 days. His son can complete the same job in 15 days. Together how many days will they take to complete the job?
questions.explanation: The work done by the man and his son in 1 day is 1/10 + 1/15 = 1/6.

questions.question: A tank is filled by pipe A in 12 hours. Pipe B can fill the same tank in 18 hours. If both the pipes are opened simultaneously, then how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: The work done by both pipes in 1 hour is 1/12 + 1/18 = 1/8.

questions.question: A train travels from Howrah to Sealdah in 30 minutes. If the speed of the train is 45 km/h, how far is Sealdah from Howrah?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed * Time, and convert time to hours.

questions.question: A car travels at an average speed of 60 km/h. How much time will it take to cover a distance of 120 km?
questions.explanation: Time = Distance / Speed.

questions.question: If a person travels from Kolkata to Bardhaman at an average speed of 80 km/h and returns at an average speed of 60 km/h, then what is the average speed for the whole trip?
questions.explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time.

questions.question: A bus travels from Siliguri to Jalpaiguri at an average speed of 40 km/h and takes 5 hours to cover the distance. How far is Jalpaiguri from Siliguri?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed * Time.

questions.question: A train travels at an average speed of 60 km/h for 2 hours, then at a speed of 80 km/h for another 2 hours. What is the average speed for the whole trip?
questions.explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time.

questions.question: If 2 is added to the first digit of the numbers 123, 234, 345, and so on, the numbers become 125, 236, 347, and so on. What will be the number if the same operation is performed on the first digit of the number 678?
questions.explanation: Following the pattern, 6 is replaced by 8.

questions.question: Which of the following letters will replace the letter 'T' in the word 'WELCOMET' if it is replaced by the letter which is two places ahead of it in the English Alphabet?
questions.explanation: The letter 'T' will be replaced by the letter 'W'.

questions.question: In a certain code, the letters of the word 'KOLKATA' are coded as 'K-2, O-15, L-12, K-2, A-1, T-20, A-1'. What does the code '15 1 2 20' represent?
questions.explanation: Following the given code.

questions.question: If in a certain code, the first letter of the word 'RABINDRANATH' is coded as 'R-4', then what will be the code for the first letter of the word 'GAUHATI'?
questions.explanation: Following the pattern.

questions.question: A certain code is used to code the following letters: A = 2, B = 5, C = 8, D = 1, E = 4, F = 7, G = 10, H = 3, I = 6, J = 9. What does the code '6 9 10' represent?
questions.explanation: Following the given code.

questions.question: If a person is facing north and turns 135 degrees to his right, then which direction is he facing?
questions.explanation: He is facing south.

questions.question: If a person walks 50 meters south from his house and then walks 70 meters east, then which direction is his house from his current position?
questions.explanation: His house is in the north direction.

questions.question: If a person is facing a river and wants to go to the other side, which direction should he take?
questions.explanation: He should take the west direction.

questions.question: From the Victoria Memorial in Kolkata, if a person walks 200 meters south and then 150 meters east, which direction is the Victoria Memorial from his current position?
questions.explanation: The Victoria Memorial is in the west direction from his current position.

questions.question: A person is standing at the Howrah Bridge in Kolkata. If he walks 50 meters towards the river Ganges and then 70 meters north, which direction is the Howrah Bridge from his current position?
questions.explanation: The Howrah Bridge is in the south direction from his current position.

questions.question: Find the next term in the series: 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, 16, ?
questions.explanation: The series is formed by adding consecutive prime numbers.

questions.question: Identify the pattern in the given series of letters: A, D, G, J, M, ?
questions.explanation: The pattern is formed by adding 3 letters to the previous term in the English alphabet.

questions.question: In the following series, find the missing term: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.explanation: The series is formed by adding consecutive even numbers to the previous term.

questions.question: Find the next term in the series: B, E, H, K, N, ?
questions.explanation: The series is formed by adding 3 letters to the previous term in the English alphabet.

questions.question: In the given series, find the next number: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, ?
questions.explanation: The series is formed by multiplying the previous term by 2.
